successCriteria.description: File `src/shared/types/leave.types.ts` exists and exports all 5 enums with exact values: LeaveStatus (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED), EmploymentStatus (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMINATED, ON_LEAVE), AuditAction (CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED, SUBMITTED), NotificationType (LEAVE_REQUEST_SUBMITTED, LEAVE_APPROVED, LEAVE_REJECTED, LEAVE_CANCELLED, BALANCE_UPDATED), EntityType (EMPLOYEE, LEAVE_TYPE, LEAVE_POLICY, LEAVE_REQUEST, LEAVE_BALANCE, AUDIT_LOG, NOTIFICATION)

File `src/shared/error.types.ts` exists and exports all 5 domain error classes: NotFoundError (extends Error, constructor takes entityName: string, id: string, exposes entityName and id as public readonly properties), ValidationError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string, details?: unknown[], exposes details as public readonly property), ConflictError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string), UnauthorizedError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string), ForbiddenError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string)

File `tests/unit/shared/error.types.test.ts` exists with Jest unit tests covering instantiation and property access for all 5 error classes: NotFoundError (entityName and id properties), ValidationError (message and details properties, including undefined details), ConflictError (message property), UnauthorizedError (message property), ForbiddenError (message property)
TypeScript compilation passes with strict mode (no implicit any, strict null checks) — `npm run build` succeeds
All Jest tests pass — `npx jest --passWithNoTests` exits with code 0 and the error types test suite reports all tests passing

constraints: All TypeScript enums must use the `enum` keyword (not const objects, not string literal unions) — the intent explicitly says "Enum" for each type
All error classes must extend the native `Error` class and set `this.name` to the class name for correct `instanceof` behavior

Error class constructors must match the exact signatures specified: NotFoundError(entityName: string, id: string), ValidationError(message: string, details?: unknown[]), ConflictError(message: string), UnauthorizedError(message: string), ForbiddenError(message: string)
No `any` type usage — use `unknown` with type guards instead (per HARNESS.json constraint no-any)
Strict TypeScript: no implicit any, strict null checks enabled (per HARNESS.json stack declaration)
Files must be created at exact paths: `src/shared/types/leave.types.ts`, `src/shared/error.types.ts`, `tests/unit/shared/error.types.test.ts`
outOfScope: Phase 2: Employee domain model and repository (src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts, employee.repository.ts, migrations)
Phase 3: LeaveType and LeavePolicy domain models and repositories (src/modules/leaveType/, src/modules/leavePolicy/)
Phase 4: LeaveBalance domain model and repository (src/modules/balance/)
Phase 5: LeaveRequest domain model and repository (src/modules/leave/)
Phase 6: AuditLog and Notification domain models and repositories (src/modules/audit/, src/modules/notification/)
Phases 7-10: All service layers, controllers, routes, and Fastify plugin registrations for any module
Any database migrations, Knex configuration, or repository implementations — this phase is types-only
Any audit logging, RBAC enforcement, or input validation logic — these are cross-cutting concerns for later phases

ambiguities.description: ARCHITECTURE.md line 25 lists `LeaveType` as an export from `src/shared/types/leave.types.ts`, but the Phase 1 intent does not include a `LeaveType` enum. The DOMAIN.md defines `LeaveType` as a set of string literal values (annual, sick, emergency, unpaid, maternity, paternity) under the policy section rather than as a shared enum. Phase 3 creates `LeaveType` as a domain model in `src/modules/leaveType/`. The ARCHITECTURE.md reference may be stale or anticipating a future re-export.
options: Follow the Phase 1 intent exactly — do not create a LeaveType enum in leave.types.ts. Phase 3 will define LeaveType as a domain model.
Add a LeaveType string enum to leave.types.ts to match ARCHITECTURE.md, with values: ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY, UNPAID, MATERNITY, PATERNITY.
